cards.name: Rootling
cards.image_prompt: Create a vertical 3:4 fantasy card illustration of Rootling, a small lively treant sprout with a monstrous wooden face formed from crooked knots and a jagged hollow mouth, tiny white-quartz growths, fresh leaves, and a soft green healing glow. Give it a playful but brave pose. Its face must look naturally grown and distinctly non-human, with no human nose, lips, skin, or facial proportions. Use a stylized low-poly fantasy art direction with clean polygonal forms and crisp planar shading: chunky proportions, a bold readable silhouette, faceted low-poly bark and crystal surfaces, warm cinematic lighting, and a simple parchment-toned forest backdrop. Keep the face and main silhouette within the upper 80% of the image. Use the lowest 20% for root feet standing on a mossy fallen branch and subtle forest ground, keeping it attractive when the full image is shown but safe beneath a translucent description panel. Family-friendly, no humans, no photorealism, no text, no logo, no border, no card frame, no UI.

Use a stylized low-poly fantasy art direction with clean polygonal forms and crisp planar shading: agile exaggerated proportions, a bold readable silhouette, faceted low-poly natural materials, warm light, cool crystal accents, and a simple parchment-toned woodland backdrop. cards.name: Crystal Vent
cards.image_prompt: Create a vertical 3:4 fantasy spell-card illustration of Crystal Vent: a white-quartz charge stone set in an old root-and-rock mechanism cracks open and releases a bright cyan-white burst of crystal energy. A second socket glows nearby while the spent socket is dark. Use a stylized low-poly fantasy art direction with clean polygonal forms, crisp planar shading, faceted stone and crystal, warm shadows, and a simple forest-shrine backdrop. Compose the artwork for a circular crop: place the cracked crystal, both sockets, and the complete magical burst together at the exact center of the image, contained within an imaginary central circle. Let the round mechanism create a natural visual cradle around the effect. Keep the corners and outer edges quiet, with no important details outside the central circle. Do not draw a visible circle. Family-friendly, no characters, no photorealism, no text, no symbols, no logo, no border, no card frame, no UI.

cards.name: Crystal Growth

Use a stylized low-poly fantasy art direction with clean polygonal forms, crisp planar shading, faceted bark and crystal, vibrant magical light, and a restrained forest backdrop. Compose the artwork for a circular crop: center the creature's head and torso, new armor, vines, and complete crystal-growth effect together inside an imaginary circle in the middle of the image. Curve the magical growth inward so its silhouette remains readable within the circle. Keep the corners and outer edges quiet, with no important details outside the central circle.
